assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'PodDisruptionBudgets (Most Common)' and mentions PDBs as 'the most likely culprit' in the conclusion

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Multiple commands provided: 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' appears several times throughout the response

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Provides multiple commands including 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep Terminating' and commands to check pods without controllers

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Does not mention resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to relax PDBs: 'kubectl patch pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' and kubectl delete pdb command

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic force-drain after 1 hour, nor GKE notifications for PDB-blocked evictions

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Section 6 provides surge upgrade configuration with specific command: 'gcloud container node-pools update ... --max-surge-upgrade 3 --max-unavailable-upgrade 1'

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention webhooks or admission controllers as potential causes of stuck pod drainage

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Does not provide specific steps to validate that fixes are working or that the upgrade is proceeding after applying solutions

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Does not mention POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, Cloud Logging, or Pub/Sub notifications for PDB-blocked evictions
